currentSliceNotes: Re-scoped active RMG work away from owner-H3M sample-by-sample exact-count fitting. Added docs/native-rmg-generalized-policy-regate-audit.md, which records exact counts as diagnostics only, identifies owner-specific runtime branches as temporary fixture/diagnostic debt, and defines the next implementation direction around generalized template/profile policy, zone graph semantics, town/guard/blocker route closure, road materialization, water/underground policy, and corpus subsystem grouping. Earlier local Small-islands count-fitting edits were abandoned before commit; uploaded/generated map evidence remains untracked. Owner-corpus report schema v6 now emits generalized_policy_failure_summary alongside raw exact deltas; after native rebuild, the raw mapped gate remained 8/9 while the active Small islands failure was grouped into guard_policy, town_policy, decoration_blocker_policy, object_density_policy, and object_reward_policy. Owner-requested map-editor generated-package inspection hotfix completed: editor index now includes loadable generated packages rejected by skirmish launch validation as Inspect Only; skirmish launch filtering remains strict. Runtime policy classification now exposes generalized policy keys plus owner-runtime override debt in generated output/report/provenance/metadata; auto-template batch schema v2 passed with zero seed-specific override activation for normal generated seeds. Generated-map setup now exposes explicit `Surface Only (1 Level)` and `Surface + Underground (2 Levels)` level options, islands no longer hide the two-level option, and one-level scoped islands terrain no longer injects underground terrain onto the surface. The Small normal-water two-level owner-corpus package path now preserves player start towns on their generated start anchors during owner spacing reflow. Native town placement now carries level-aware occupancy/spacing and places the owner Small normal-water supplemental fifth town underground; owner-corpus schema v6 improved to 7/9 mapped pass, with Small islands two-level and XL no-water remaining. Added fast non-Godot RMG audit tooling so H3M/native package validation no longer depends on a full Godot startup; Godot remains for generation/adoption/editor/runtime integration only. Small islands two-level work also corrected level-aware object/guard/town pathing defects exposed by the focused diagnostic, while broad production parity remains in progress. Focused Small islands two-level validation now passes in a narrow Godot integration smoke; the slow full Godot owner-corpus parser loop is no longer the recommended tight validation path. Fast audit batch mode now parses the whole uploaded owner H3M corpus and local native AMAP evidence without Godot, providing a corpus-level baseline for the next generalized RMG policy fixes. Native batch export now isolates the single Godot-required generation step from Python-side corpus comparison; the resulting generated batch confirms broad object-density underfill on large two-level maps and sets the next generalized policy target. Fast validation wrapper added so the default post-export loop now fails quickly on generated-map rules and broad policy gaps in Python: the current native batch reports empty underground levels, missing underground roads, near-stacked towns, and object-density underfill without running a Godot parser scene. Generalized catalog-auto generation now addresses those failures with size/level-aware density floors, eligible underground decoration/scenic and road distribution, strict town-spacing/same-zone town guards, and a targeted batch-export case filter; a full 18-case owner-file-derived export now validates with zero export failures, zero fast native-rule failures, and zero density gaps. The fast validator now has a broader policy gate for category density, road density, and guard-to-reward ratio floors; the same 18-case batch correctly fails that broader production-shape gate with 14 policy gaps. Native catalog-auto scenic-object and guard floors reduce the broader policy gaps to 5 on combined 18-package evidence; the generated guard floor raises stale fixture caps for normal non-owner-discovered catalog-auto packages, and the Small two-level road-density floor now grows connected surface/underground roads. A tiny policy density epsilon clears sub-tile road-density noise without hiding material town gaps. Generated two-level neutral-town floors now clear the remaining Large/Small town-density gaps while avoiding used town zones and preserving route closure. The fast validator now adds a road-topology gate; normal generated catalog-auto road overlays now use generalized separated component materialization by size and level count, and fresh 18-package evidence passes with 0 parse/native/density/policy/topology gaps. Full production RMG parity remains in progress. Medium two-level islands now has a scoped road/density/terrain/blocker-preservation profile; the merged 18-case evidence remains fast-gate clean while production parity stays false due remaining exact-shape gaps. Medium two-level normal-water now has scoped road, object/category, reward, guard, terrain, and town-spacing profile corrections; the merged 18-case evidence remains fast-gate clean while production parity stays false due remaining exact-shape gaps. XL one-level islands now has scoped road, reward, guard, and island land-fraction profile corrections; the merged 18-case evidence remains fast-gate clean while production parity stays false due remaining town/route/road-component shape gaps.
nextActionGuardrailNote: Do not commit uploaded .h3m/.amap/.ascenario evidence files; use them only as local comparison inputs.

classificationRules.completed: Implementation or documentation-only governance work satisfies the PLAN completion criteria and has validation/evidence.
classificationRules.docs_ready: Source docs, plans, or reports exist, but the implementation slice is not complete.
classificationRules.paused: Deliberately stopped or deferred until a future explicit slice or owner decision selects it.
classificationRules.superseded: Replaced by a later accepted plan, slice, or implementation path; preserved as evidence only.
classificationRules.pending_after_implementation: Follow-up review that should wait for the named implementation output.

invalidArchivedSemantics.report_only: Encoded as status docs_ready with classification report_only when the helper status set requires valid statuses.
invalidArchivedSemantics.deferred: Encoded as status paused with deferredReason when the helper status set requires valid statuses.

reconciledHistorySummary.sourceOfTruth: project.md, PLAN.md, and ops/progress.json after progress architecture reconciliation on 2026-04-27.
reconciledHistorySummary.archiveRole: archive/document-reset-2026-04-27 is evidence/history only, not the active worker queue.
reconciledHistorySummary.implementationTruth: Many archived Phase 2 docs, reports, tooling, UI, terrain/editor, object metadata, economy proof, and strategic AI report harness slices were completed. They are grouped below as evidence and do not automatically complete broad Phase 2 parents.
reconciledHistorySummary.activeQueueRule: plannedSlices contains current executable parent and child slices. The active continuation is Phase 2 RMG parity recovery; completed non-RMG/future-phase slices remain evidence only and must not become the current queue.

plannedSlices.title: Native RMG Generalized Policy Re-Gate
plannedSlices.purpose: Stop the owner-H3M sample-by-sample exact-count fitting loop and re-gate native RMG around generalized recovered-template policy stages that owner corpus samples validate instead of selecting.
baselineChecks: python3 -m py_compile tools/rmg_fast_audit.py tools/rmg_fast_validation.py tools/rmg_python_validation_gate.py tools/rmg_export_timing_summary.py
completionCriteria: The corrective audit clearly distinguishes owner-H3M exact-count diagnostics from production RMG policy.
Current sample-specific runtime branches are identified as temporary fixture/diagnostic debt, not the desired architecture.
The next implementation direction is defined around generalized template/profile policy, zone graph semantics, town/guard/blocker route closure, road materialization, and corpus subsystem grouping.
No uploaded .h3m, generated .amap, or generated .ascenario evidence is committed.
The owner-corpus report groups mapped comparison failures by generalized subsystem in addition to preserving raw exact deltas.
nonGoals: No production-ready RMG claim.
No new one-off owner sample count fitting as runtime policy.
No HoMM3 copyrighted asset or DEF import.
validationResults: Local overfit Small-islands count-fitting changes were abandoned before commit; the code worktree returned to the pushed checkpoint state.
docs/native-rmg-generalized-policy-regate-audit.md records why the exact-count loop is diagnostic-only and why the next implementation must generalize.

tests/native_random_map_homm3_owner_corpus_coverage_report.tscn emitted schema native_random_map_homm3_owner_corpus_coverage_report_v6; the raw gate remains 8/9 mapped pass, while generalized_policy_failure_summary groups the active Small islands failure into guard_policy, town_policy, decoration_blocker_policy, object_density_policy, and object_reward_policy.

MapPackageService.generate_random_map now emits runtime_policy_classification in generated output, validation reports, provenance, and map metadata, exposing generalized policy keys and active owner-runtime override debt.

tests/native_random_map_auto_template_batch_report.tscn passed with schema native_random_map_auto_template_batch_report_v2; representative generated cases carry runtime policy classification and seed_specific_runtime_override_case_count is 0 for normal generated seeds.

Generated-map setup now exposes explicit Surface Only (1 Level) and Surface + Underground (2 Levels) level options; random_map_player_setup_retry_ux_report passed with visible level_count control data.

Native one-level scoped islands terrain no longer injects underground terrain onto the surface, while scoped two-level requests materialize an underground layer; native_random_map_terrain_grid_report passed with both checks.

Small normal-water two-level owner-corpus package validation now preserves player start towns on their generated start anchors during owner spacing reflow; native_random_map_owner_normal_water_underground_package_report passed with map_level_count 2 and validation_status pass.

Explicit level-label correction validation passed: random_map_player_setup_retry_ux_report reported level_options [Surface Only (1 Level), Surface + Underground (2 Levels)], native_random_map_terrain_grid_report passed, tests/validate_repo.py passed, git diff --check passed, and jq empty ops/progress.json passed.

Native town placement now uses level-aware primary occupancy keys and same-level spacing comparisons; focused debug inspection for owner_discovered_s_2playerss_normalwater_2level produced five towns with four on surface and one underground while preserving the 14-tile same-level spacing floor.

tests/native_random_map_homm3_owner_corpus_coverage_report.tscn emitted schema native_random_map_homm3_owner_corpus_coverage_report_v6 after the level-aware town placement fix; the raw mapped gate remains fail overall but improved to 7/9 mapped pass, with the previous Small normal-water two-level failure gone and remaining mapped failures limited to Small islands two-level and XL no-water.

Owner-requested fast audit path added: tools/rmg_fast_audit.py parses gzip/raw owner .h3m evidence and native .amap JSON packages directly for category/level counts, road component topology, and town-route semantic summaries without starting Godot.

Fast audit validation passed locally: py_compile passed; Small islands two-level owner .h3m parsed in about 0.067s with decoration 116, guard 37, object 56, reward 102, town 8, road cells 147, and road topology surface [45, 37, 15, 11, 10], underground [29]; a native medium .amap parsed in about 0.086s; XL no-water owner .h3m parsed in about 1.149s.

Testing policy corrected: Python CLI audit/comparison is now the default tight loop for parsed .h3m/.amap evidence; Godot report scenes remain final integration gates for native GDExtension generation, package conversion/adoption, editor loading, and runtime behavior.

Focused Small islands two-level diagnosis found underground roads were materialized while town/object/guard/reward records stayed effectively surface-only; native object and guard placement now preserve point.level with level-aware occupancy/pathing keys, and the Small islands two-level path materializes an underground category distribution for the current owner-corpus comparison.

Focused Small islands two-level integration smoke passed after level-aware pathing correction: .artifacts/focused_owner_small_islands_debug.tscn reported object/town/guard/road deltas 0, category deltas 0, road topology surface [45, 37, 15, 11, 10], underground [29], validation_status pass, and semantic_layout_match with native_guarded_route_reachable_pair_count_total 0.

Fast audit batch mode added and validated: --h3m-dir maps/h3m-maps --allow-failures parsed all 18 uploaded owner H3M files in about 5.236s with 0 parse failures, and --amap-dir maps --allow-failures parsed all 5 local native AMAP evidence packages in about 0.204s with 0 parse failures.

Native batch export harness added and validated: tools/rmg_native_batch_export.tscn generated and saved 18 owner-file-derived native AMAP packages with 0 export failures; tools/rmg_fast_audit.py parsed those 18 generated packages in about 3.208s with 0 parse failures. The batch exposes the broad density gap: owner 108x108 two-level averages 169.725 objects/1000 tiles while native averages 45.882, and owner 144x144 two-level averages 93.195 while native averages 44.456.

Fast validation wrapper added: tools/rmg_fast_validation.py parsed all 18 owner H3Ms without Godot in about 5.199s. Against the current 18 generated native AMAP packages, it completed in about 8.118s total and correctly failed the diagnostic gate with 32 native rule failures plus 6 density gaps, including empty underground levels, missing underground roads, near-stacked towns, and object-density underfill.

Generalized generated-batch rule fixes added: native catalog-auto generation now uses size/level-aware object density floors, copies only eligible decoration/scenic objects underground, materializes deterministic underground road cells for two-level packages, and enforces size-aware town spacing while skipping or deferring extra non-player same-zone towns. tools/rmg_native_batch_export.gd now accepts --case filtering for targeted Godot regeneration. A full owner-file-derived export wrote 18/18 packages with 0 export failures, and tools/rmg_fast_validation.py parsed 18 owner H3Ms plus those 18 AMAPs in about 8.535s with 0 parse failures, 0 density gaps, and 0 native rule failures.

Fast validation policy gate added: tools/rmg_fast_validation.py now fails broad category-density, road-density, and guard-to-reward underfill against owner group baselines without exact per-sample count matching. The full 18-case batch now reports status fail with 14 policy gaps, 0 parse failures, 0 native rule failures, and 0 density gaps; --no-policy-gate preserves the narrower generated-rule-only pass.

Native catalog-auto scenic-object and guard floors added: combined 18-package evidence from partial full export plus targeted remaining XL export parses in about 8.829s with 0 parse failures, 0 native rule failures, and 0 density gaps. The broader policy gate now reports 5 gaps instead of 14; all object/scenic category-density gaps are gone, while remaining gaps are town density, Small two-level road density, a near-threshold XL one-level road floor, and Small one-level guard/reward ratio.

Generated catalog-auto guard floor cap fix added: normal non-owner-discovered catalog-auto packages now let the generalized reward-ratio guard floor raise stale fixture caps. Targeted Small one-level export s_randomnumberofplayers now reports 47 guards for 80 rewards, guarded reachable town pairs remain 0, targeted fast validation passes with 0 policy gaps, and the combined 18-package Python policy gate drops from 5 gaps to 4 with 0 parse failures, 0 native rule failures, and 0 density gaps.

Small two-level road floor added: normal generated Small two-level catalog-auto packages now grow connected road clusters across surface and underground instead of relying on sparse underground copies. Targeted Small normal-water and islands two-level exports each report 116 road cells with 87 on surface and 29 underground, guarded reachable town pairs remain 0, targeted fast validation clears the 36x36_l2 road-density policy gap, and the combined 18-package Python policy gate drops from 4 gaps to 3 with 0 parse failures, 0 native rule failures, and 0 density gaps.

Fast policy density epsilon added: tools/rmg_fast_validation.py now uses a 0.05 per-1000-tile epsilon for category and road policy density checks so sub-tile rounding noise does not drive generator work. The near-threshold 144x144_l1 road gap is gone, while the 108x108_l2 and 36x36_l2 town-density gaps remain; combined 18-package validation reports 2 policy gaps, 0 parse failures, 0 native rule failures, and 0 density gaps.

Generated two-level neutral-town floor added: normal non-owner-discovered catalog-auto two-level maps now materialize neutral towns to a generalized size/level-aware floor with recovered +0x30 neutral_minimum_towns semantics. Small two-level supplements remain underground where valid; Large two-level supplements use surface source-zone placement to avoid open unguarded underground town routes. Targeted 5-case export passed with 0 failures, targeted fast validation passed with 0 policy gaps, and the combined 18-package Python policy gate now passes with 0 parse failures, 0 native rule failures, 0 density gaps, and 0 policy gaps.

Fast road-topology gate added: tools/rmg_fast_validation.py now fails on largest road-component dominance and underbranched component count against matched owner evidence without requiring exact component arrays. Current combined 18-package evidence reports 19 topology gaps with 0 parse failures, 0 native rule failures, 0 density gaps, and 0 category/road/guard-reward policy gaps; --no-topology-gate preserves the previous pass. This identifies generalized road component materialization as the next production blocker.

Generalized catalog-auto road component materialization added: normal non-owner-corpus generated road overlays now replace the serialized all-routes trunk with deterministic separated components by size and level count while preserving route graph reachability and connection guard controls. A fresh 18-package export plus targeted xl_nowater no-stacking re-export passed Python validation with 18 matched comparisons, 0 parse failures, 0 native rule failures, 0 density gaps, 0 policy gaps, and 0 topology gaps.

Normal generated Large/XL land seeds no longer activate owner-specific land density/profile override branches; those branches are now limited to explicit production-parity audit seeds. The XL one-level road floor now scales from area before component splitting, keeping 144x144_l1 above the Python policy floor without the XL owner branch. The generated 18-package fast validation remains green with 0 parse/native/density/policy/topology gaps, runtime override scanning now shows only the Small 049 owner profile override debt in the normal batch, and the Godot auto-template integration report now mirrors native town-spacing floors while treating Medium normal-water owner counts as floors instead of exact equality.

Generalized object-category floor added: normal catalog-auto scenic/other-object floors now scale by size and level before decorative filler, and the Python policy gate now requires the native object category to reach at least 60% of matched owner group density instead of 25%. A fresh 18-package export passed with 0 failures, fast validation passed with 0 parse/native/density/policy/topology gaps, and object-category totals improved across every group, including 108x108_l1 243 -> 558, 108x108_l2 955 -> 1624, 144x144_l1 540 -> 1260, and 144x144_l2 1097 -> 1441. The denser XL placement path makes export time about eight and a half minutes, so placement-loop profiling is the next performance target.

Fast validation workflow tightened after owner review: tools/rmg_fast_validation.py now supports --latest-amap-artifact and --summary so the default RMG comparison loop validates the newest generated AMAP batch with one compact Python command instead of rerunning Godot parser/report scenes. The current latest batch selected .artifacts/rmg_native_batch_export_after_object_category_floor, parsed 18/18 owner H3Ms plus 18/18 native AMAPs in about 8.458s, matched 18 comparisons, and reported status=pass with 0 parse/native/density/policy/topology gaps. Fresh package generation/export remains the Godot-only boundary until a separate native CLI exists.

Town/guard placement profiling and fast path added: native_random_map_extension_profile_report now exposes town/guard subphase timings, boundary opening cover batches opening cells by nearest guard instead of duplicating/signing guards per cell, and town-pair route closure uses cached town visit cells plus per-pass connected components to skip full pathfinding for already disconnected pairs. Representative profile wall times improved from about 13.2s -> 6.4s, 9.5s -> 4.3s, and 9.7s -> 7.4s. A fresh 18-package export passed with 0 failures in real 476.65s, and Python fast validation over that batch reported status=pass with 0 parse/native/density/policy/topology gaps. Generation remains too slow for final production; object placement and full XL export time remain active targets.

Python-only RMG gate and manifest timing summary added after owner review: tools/rmg_python_validation_gate.py now runs parser syntax checks plus fast H3M/AMAP validation/comparison without starting Godot, and tools/rmg_export_timing_summary.py ranks native batch export manifests by case and phase timing. tools/rmg_native_batch_export.gd now writes per-case generation/conversion/save/case wall timings plus compact native profile top phases. A timed full export wrote 18/18 packages with 0 failures in real 480.73s; the Python gate selected that latest batch and passed in about 8.865s with 0 parse/native/density/policy/topology gaps. Timing summary shows xl_nowater_2levels as the current worst case at 74.628s, dominated by 37.880s package conversion plus native object_placement as the top generation phase at about 15.579s.

Native package conversion profiling and fast path added: build_native_package_session_adoption now returns compact conversion_profile phases, broad land-boundary choke mask adoption batches boundary cells by nearest decorative object instead of repeatedly mutating object dictionaries, and rmg_native_batch_export uses save_map_package return_package=false to avoid a large save-result payload. Targeted xl_nowater_2levels improved from the timed baseline 74.628s case / 37.880s conversion to about 40.982s case / 5.567s conversion, while a focused Large/XL land export wrote 4/4 packages with 0 failures. Python fast validation over that focused batch passed in about 6.238s with 0 parse/native/density/policy/topology gaps, and timing summary now shows native object_placement as the remaining dominant phase.

Python full-gate coverage tightened: rmg_fast_validation now supports --require-all-owner-matches, and rmg_python_validation_gate enables that coverage check by default so a targeted partial native AMAP export cannot be mistaken for a full owner-corpus correctness gate. The focused 4-case land batch still passes ordinary targeted validation, while the same batch with --require-all-owner-matches correctly reports a coverage gap for the 14 missing owner cases.

Native object-placement and occupancy profiling fast path added: object placement records now use compact deterministic signatures, combined occupancy signatures use compact record keys/counts, no-op corridor/choke clearance skips full object-placement rehashing, town access corridor clearance is level-aware, and rmg_native_batch_export manifests include top profile phases. Targeted xl_nowater_2levels improved to about 36.115s case time with 24.484s generation, 5.879s conversion, and 5.752s save; Python fast validation over that targeted AMAP passed in about 5.632s with 0 parse/native/density/policy/topology gaps. The object-placement report now counts only MapDocument records tagged native_record_kind=object_placement when comparing against the placement payload, because MapDocument correctly also includes towns, guards, and gates. Focused object-placement and broader auto-template Godot integration smokes passed. The testing split remains explicit: Python validates and compares H3M/AMAP outputs, while Godot is reserved for fresh generation/export and actual editor/runtime smokes.

Guard-mediated closure diagnostic gate added: rmg_fast_audit now separates native guard body tiles from guard control-zone tiles when computing object-only town-route topology, and rmg_fast_validation exposes optional --closure-shape-gate. Default full fast validation over .artifacts/rmg_native_batch_export_timing_full still passes with 0 parse/native/density/policy/topology/coverage gaps, while the optional closure-shape gate reports 13 missing_guard_mediated_town_route_closure gaps. This identifies the next production blocker: native maps often close town routes through permanent terrain/blocker shape before guards matter, while owner H3M evidence often has openings that guards close.

Python full correctness gate tightened: rmg_python_validation_gate now enables the guard-mediated closure-shape gate by default and exposes --no-closure-shape-gate only for targeted diagnostics. A targeted l_nowater_randomplayers_nounder no-land-rock-barrier probe still produced 0 object-only reachable town pairs and 0 guarded reachable town pairs, so the current closure blocker is object/body mask shape as well as terrain shape. This keeps the blocker visible through the fast Python H3M/AMAP path without starting Godot.

Guard-mediated package route shape implemented: package adoption now narrows guard package_body_tiles to the primary stack tile, keeps route/control closure in package_block_tiles, cuts only decorative/scenic blocker masks along terrain-valid town-pair corridors, and assigns nearby guard closure masks. Focused 2-case and 13-case exports passed the Python closure-shape gate, and a fresh combined 18/18 owner/native AMAP evidence set passed rmg_python_validation_gate with 0 parse/native/density/policy/topology/coverage/closure-shape gaps in about 11.414s parse time.

Python RMG validation and timing workflow tightened: rmg_python_validation_gate now includes native export timing manifest summaries without starting Godot, and rmg_export_timing_summary --latest-amap-artifact now selects the newest manifest-bearing export artifact instead of failing on manifestless combined AMAP evidence directories. The full Python gate passed with 18/18 owner/native matches, 0 parse/native/density/policy/topology/coverage/closure-shape gaps, and a clearly labeled fallback timing summary because the validated combined AMAP directory has no manifest.

Python production gap audit added: tools/rmg_production_gap_audit.py consumes the same owner H3M/native AMAP evidence and emits a prompt-to-artifact production checklist without starting Godot. The current audit passes as an audit but reports production_ready=false, 5 missing production requirements, 18/18 owner/native diagnostic mismatches, 18 road-shape mismatches, 18 severe category-gap cases, and 17 route-shape gap cases; the highest-severity blockers are XL/Large cases led by xl_islands_2levels, xl_nowater, xl_water_2levels, and l_nowater_randomplayers_nounder.

Normal generated catalog-auto land terrain barrier fallback disabled: native land maps no longer use broad terrain-rock zone boundaries for normal catalog-auto closure; package decorative/scenic blockers plus route guards carry the closure shape instead. Python audit/production-gap tooling now tracks terrain_blocked_tile_count_delta, adds terrain_blocker_shape_similarity, and includes terrain deltas in severity ranking. Native rebuild passed; a targeted 5-case land export validated with 0 parse/native/density/policy/topology/coverage/closure-shape gaps, the combined 18-case Python gate passed with 18/18 matches in about 12.219s parse time, and the production audit remains production_ready=false with 6 missing requirements and 15 terrain-shape gap cases. The testing boundary is explicit: Godot exports fresh packages and smokes engine/editor behavior; Python owns H3M/AMAP validation, comparison, timing summaries, and production-gap ranking.

Two-level underground distribution and guarded route closure repair added: normal generated catalog-auto two-level packages now rebalance underground object/reward records, reserve an underground share of generated neutral-town floors, and iteratively recheck guarded town routes after decorative/scenic corridor cuts so guard package masks close the remaining routes. Native rebuild passed; a focused 5-case failing export plus xl_water_2levels regenerated the affected Large/XL two-level packages, targeted Python validation passed with 0 closure-shape gaps, the combined 18-case Python gate passed with 18/18 matches and 0 parse/native/density/policy/topology/coverage/closure-shape gaps in about 13.736s parse time, and the production audit remains production_ready=false with 6 missing broad requirements.

Underground rock-shape policy added for normal generated two-level land/islands and XL normal-water profiles: terrain generation now preserves open underground cells around roads, towns, guards, objects, visit/approach tiles, and town corridors before filling remaining underground space with deterministic rock. Native rebuild passed; a focused six-case export wrote 6/6 packages, the combined 18-case Python gate passed with 18/18 matches and 0 parse/native/density/policy/topology/coverage/closure-shape gaps in about 12.937s parse time, and production audit top severity improved materially while remaining production_ready=false: xl_islands_2levels 26912 -> 18396, xl_water_2levels 24032 -> 14136, and xl_nowater_2levels 21003 -> 12389.

Road-shape generalization refined: normal catalog-auto road overlay materialization now uses profile-aware total road floors, surface/underground road splits, per-level component counts, and skewed component-size targets instead of equal-sized chunks; Small/Medium two-level scenic-object floors were raised to keep the object-category policy gate green on fresh exports. Native rebuild passed; a full 18-case export wrote 18/18 packages in about 401.618s, and the merged 18-case Python gate passed with 18/18 owner/native matches, 0 parse/native/density/policy/topology/coverage/closure-shape gaps, and about 13.033s parse time. Production audit remains production_ready=false with 6 missing broad requirements, but worst XL two-level road deltas improved to xl_islands_2levels -9, xl_water_2levels 16, and xl_nowater_2levels -9.

Focused terrain-shape profile tuning export wrote 5/5 targeted packages in about 158.647s, and the Python-only validation gate over that targeted AMAP batch passed in about 7.872s parse time with 0 parse/native/density/policy/topology/closure-shape gaps. Production gap audit now supports --allow-partial-native-batch so targeted AMAP batches no longer report a fast-gate failure just because unrelated owner cases were not regenerated. docs/rmg-python-validation-workflow.md records the split: Godot for native generation/export and editor/runtime smokes, Python for owner H3M/native AMAP parsing, comparison, validation, and production-gap diagnostics.

Full terrain-shape profile tuning checkpoint passed the generalized Python gate: a full native export wrote 18/18 AMAP packages in about 392.420s, then rmg_python_validation_gate passed with 18/18 owner/native matches, 0 parse/native/density/policy/topology/coverage/closure-shape gaps, and about 12.020s parse time with timing summary evidence. Production audit still blocks production-ready status with 6 missing broad requirements; top severity remains XL/Large terrain, route-shape, and category deltas led by xl_nowater_2levels, xl_islands_2levels, and xl_islands.

Native catalog-auto land decoration blocker floor added: targeted Large/XL land export wrote 4/4 affected packages in about 139.309s, and the merged 18-case evidence passed rmg_python_validation_gate with 18/18 owner/native matches, 0 parse/native/density/policy/topology/coverage/closure-shape gaps, and about 12.199s parse time. Production audit remains production_ready=false, but xl_nowater_2levels improved from severity 6318 to 3562, object delta -1401 -> +19, and decoration delta -1449 -> -29; xl_nowater improved from severity 4854 to 1292.

tools/rmg_quick_validation.py now performs the post-export RMG tight loop in one Python process: owner .h3m and native .amap evidence parse once, then the generalized correctness gate and production-gap comparison both run without starting Godot or reparsing the corpus in two scripts.

Two-level land catalog-auto town placement now uses the generated neutral-town floor as a cap and reserves an underground neutral-town share without moving player start towns. Focused xl_nowater_2levels evidence matches owner town split at 10 total towns, 7 surface and 3 underground; merged 18-case quick validation passed with 18/18 matches and 0 parse/native/density/policy/topology/coverage/closure-shape gaps, while production_ready remains false with 6 broad requirements still missing.

Two-level island terrain/profile tuning now prunes non-required surface zone-boundary land for under-blocked island size profiles, raises underground rock fractions for under-blocked island size classes, and restores an island-specific underground neutral-town share. Focused four-case island export passed rmg_quick_validation with 4/4 matched comparisons and 0 parse/native/density/policy/topology/coverage/closure-shape gaps; merged 18-case evidence also passed. plannedSlices.notes: Re-scoped active RMG work away from owner-H3M sample-by-sample exact-count fitting. Added docs/native-rmg-generalized-policy-regate-audit.md, which records exact counts as diagnostics only, identifies owner-specific runtime branches as temporary fixture/diagnostic debt, and defines the next implementation direction around generalized template/profile policy, zone graph semantics, town/guard/blocker route closure, road materialization, water/underground policy, and corpus subsystem grouping. Earlier local Small-islands count-fitting edits were abandoned before commit; uploaded/generated map evidence remains untracked. Owner-corpus report schema v6 now emits generalized_policy_failure_summary alongside raw exact deltas; after native rebuild, the raw mapped gate remained 8/9 while the active Small islands failure was grouped into guard_policy, town_policy, decoration_blocker_policy, object_density_policy, and object_reward_policy. Added runtime_policy_classification to native RMG output/report/provenance/metadata and updated the auto-template batch report to require it while guarding normal generated seeds against seed-specific owner overrides. Added explicit generated-map level selection using Surface Only (1 Level) and Surface + Underground (2 Levels), and corrected one-level scoped islands terrain so underground terrain appears only on requested two-level maps. Fixed the Small normal-water two-level owner-corpus package reflow so player start towns remain on their generated start anchors, then made native town placement level-aware so the owner Small normal-water two-level path preserves four surface towns plus one underground supplemental town. The owner corpus now improves to 7/9 mapped pass; remaining mapped failures are Small islands two-level and XL no-water. Added fast non-Godot RMG audit tooling so H3M/native package validation no longer depends on a full Godot startup; Godot remains for generation/adoption/editor/runtime integration only. Small islands two-level work also corrected level-aware object/guard/town pathing defects exposed by the focused diagnostic, while broad production parity remains in progress. Focused Small islands two-level validation now passes in a narrow Godot integration smoke; the slow full Godot owner-corpus parser loop is no longer the recommended tight validation path. Fast audit batch mode now parses the whole uploaded owner H3M corpus and local native AMAP evidence without Godot, providing a corpus-level baseline for the next generalized RMG policy fixes. Native batch export now isolates the single Godot-required generation step from Python-side corpus comparison; the resulting generated batch confirms broad object-density underfill on large two-level maps and sets the next generalized policy target. Generalized catalog-auto generation now fixes the first generated-batch rule failures with size/level-aware density floors, eligible underground decoration/scenic and road distribution, strict town-spacing/same-zone town guards, and targeted batch export; a full 18-case export passes the fast Python generated-rule gate with zero density gaps and zero native rule failures. A broader fast policy gate now fails that same batch with 14 guard/object/town/road/guard-reward policy gaps, keeping the active slice correctly in progress instead of accepting structural package validity as production readiness. Native catalog-auto scenic-object and guard floors reduce that policy gap count to 5 on combined 18-package evidence; the generated guard floor now also raises stale fixture caps for normal non-owner-discovered catalog-auto packages, the Small two-level road floor raises road density to 116 cells per targeted package, and the fast policy epsilon clears sub-tile road-density noise. Generated two-level neutral-town floors clear the remaining density/category policy gaps. The fast validator now adds a road-topology gate; generalized road component materialization now clears that gate on fresh 18-package evidence with 0 topology gaps. Normal generated Large/XL land seeds no longer activate owner-specific land profile overrides; the XL one-level road floor now keeps 144x144_l1 above the fast policy floor without that override. The object-category floor now raises normal catalog-auto scenic/other-object density before decorative filler, and the fast policy gate requires at least 60% of owner object-category density. The Godot auto-template report is retained as an integration smoke, but slow Godot parser/comparison reports are not part of the tight correctness loop. Full production parity remains in progress.

plannedSlices.title: Map/Scenario GDExtension Persistence Foundation
sourceDocs: 2026-05-03 owner direction: maps/scenarios need structure and persistence; prefer C++ Godot GDExtension map system
plannedSlices.purpose: Define and then implement a typed, durable map/scenario persistence architecture so authored and generated maps are first-class assets instead of loose JSON/dictionary payloads embedded into scenario content and session saves.
currentInspectionFindings: RandomMapGeneratorRules.generate(input_config) returns large nested Dictionary payloads with scenario_record, metadata, staging, validation/report, and provenance rather than a typed map object.

ScenarioSelectRules launches generated skirmishes by handing the generated payload to ScenarioFactory.create_generated_skirmish_session(...), preserving no-authored-writeback and memory/session-only boundaries.
Authored scenarios are bundled in content/scenarios.json with scenario metadata, terrain rows, objectives, script hooks, and placements in one JSON domain.
SaveService._save_raw_dictionary() serializes full payload dictionaries with JSON.stringify(payload, "\t") and writes raw JSON strings through FileAccess.
A Small 36x36 generated map profile wrote about 6.95 MB of JSON and measured roughly 202-219 ms in the save path, so full-payload JSON saves are already expensive before larger maps.
implementationTargets: future C++ Godot GDExtension map package module and GDScript adapter API
completionCriteria: Define typed MapDocument and ScenarioDocument boundaries with explicit schema/version, stable ids, metadata, terrain/layer data, object placements, route/validation metadata, and generated provenance.
Select a durable map package format for authored and generated maps with load, validate, save, migrate, corruption/tamper handling, and deterministic identity/hash support.
Plan save files around immutable map package references plus compact mutable session deltas rather than full map/scenario JSON rewrites where practical.
Plan content/scenarios.json migration toward a compact manifest/index with separate authored/generated map package files.
Define a staged bridge from current GDScript RMG dictionary output into the new package format before any full C++ generator rewrite.
Name rollback, compatibility, validation scene/report, and performance acceptance gates before implementation begins.
baselineChecks: focused generated-map save/load, scenario-load, and RMG validation smokes selected at kickoff
nonGoals: No immediate coding or coding-agent implementation during planning refinement.
No breaking existing saves or authored scenarios without an explicit migration slice.
No full RMG rewrite as the first step.
No renderer, fog, pathing, occupancy, gameplay semantics, or production content migration unless separately selected.
No generated PNG/asset ingestion.
plannedSlices.notes: Native inert package API, Windows config/helper, and first native RMG foundation API/identity/stub proof are implemented and validated. 2026-05-03 Windows helper correction complete: build_map_persistence_windows.bat now checks Godot --version for 4.6.2 before focused smokes and documents --allow-other-godot-version for exceptional local testing. Validation passed: python3 -m json.tool ops/progress.json; progress sync-plan --dry-run; cmake native rebuild; map package smoke; native RMG smoke; git diff --check; python3 tests/validate_repo.py. Broad package read/write/conversion/adoption, save migration, content migration, full native RMG parity, and runtime call-site adoption remain future slices.

documentationEvidence.summary: Defines current-state audit, typed MapDocument/ScenarioDocument boundaries, C++ GDExtension API, durable chunked package format, load/validate/save/migrate lifecycle, RMG bridge, staged implementation plan, validation gates, performance criteria, and explicit non-goals.

plannedSlices.title: Overworld Incremental Route Preview Refresh
completionCriteria: OverworldShell has an explicit lightweight refresh request/dirty-phase model covering map_view, action_rails, hero_actions, context_actions, route_preview/context_route, spell/specialty/artifact rails, status surfaces, save surface, and generated surfaces.

Selected route/tile preview uses a targeted refresh entrypoint that updates route/map overlay, destination-only context/primary action, and selected-tile route text without rebuilding broad context actions, hero actions, tooltip/context drawers, or unrelated rails unless dirty.
Route confirmation preserves movement/pathing semantics while reusing the selected-route cache and inspecting only the final destination interaction when the selected route remains valid.
Full refresh remains available for session/load/map/topology/mutation-heavy operations.

Profile JSONL and validation snapshots expose incremental refresh request/phase counters plus selected context, selected route destination action, route decision, skip, destination interaction counters, and cached-route execution/fallback details.

Normal selected-route confirmation uses the cached route/preview execution path without full route-wide movement revalidation, while stale or invalid selected-route caches fall back to try_move_along_route.

Focused regressions prove selected-route preview and route confirmation do not rebuild broad context actions, hero actions, broad map/topology/object/session recap signatures, full movement revalidation, or tooltip drawers while existing cache regressions and profile-log behavior still pass.

Cached selected-route open-tile confirmations update position, movement, fog, and active hero once without scenario evaluation, post-action recap, broad active-tile discovery, post-action tile context scans, or full route revalidation.

Cached selected-route interactable destinations dispatch by compact destination descriptor kind/id/tile, while missing, stale, unknown, or mismatched descriptors fall back safely to try_move_along_route.
Movement-rule profile details expose cached_execution_mode, post_action_recap_skipped, scenario_eval_skipped, interaction_dispatch_mode, and exact fallback_reason.

Open/current selected-route preview, confirmation refresh, and current-tile post-move route action surfaces use the simple-route UI fast path from cached selected-route state and destination descriptor, skipping rich route decision, route tooltip, decision brief, route target handoff, town handoff, and primary commit-check surfaces.
nonGoals: Do not implement a full async rewrite, worker threads, broad deferred scheduling, generated map content/density changes, renderer/fog behavior changes, save schema changes, object contract changes, or F3 overlay/profile-log regressions.
Do not change stale/missing/non-selected route safety semantics; keep the full try_move_along_route fallback.

completionEvidence: OverworldShell now defines refresh request phases for map_view, action_rails, hero_actions, context_actions, route_preview, spell_rails, specialty_rails, artifact_rails, status_surfaces, save_surface, and generated_surfaces.

_set_selected_tile marks only map_view and route_preview dirty for non-current selected route destinations, and route selection calls _refresh_selected_route_preview instead of the full _refresh path.

_refresh_selected_route_preview uses _refresh_selected_route_action_surface and _selected_route_destination_actions to build hold, blocked, advance, march, and destination interaction labels from the selected route destination tile only.

Route confirmation with a preserved selected route now refreshes through route_execution_changed instead of forcing a full refresh, so the cached selected route is reused and broad context/hero action surfaces stay out of the hot path.

Selected-route preview skips broad context actions, hero actions, tooltip/context drawer rebuilds, spell, specialty, artifact, save, generated, broad status surfaces, full map/topology signatures, hero/resource serialization, and objective recap construction.

Selected-route action and decision signatures are compact destination-only strings keyed by session identity, active hero id/position, movement, selected tile, route state generation, destination blocked state, and destination-tile interaction state.

Normal selected-route confirmation uses OverworldRules.execute_prevalidated_route with cached preview reachable_steps/movement_after and records cached_route_execution=true with route_validation_mode=cached_prevalidated.
Stale selected route caches keep the safe try_move_along_route fallback with cached_route_execution=false, fallback_reason, and route_validation_mode=full_revalidation.

Profile snapshots and JSONL records now include incremental_refresh request/phase data plus hero_actions, selected_context_actions, selected_route_destination_action, selected_route_decision_surface, route_destination_only_action, skipped-work counters, and movement_rules cached-route execution details.
Selected-route state now stores a compact destination_interaction_descriptor alongside the cached route and movement preview.

OverworldRules.execute_prevalidated_route uses the descriptor to run open_fast_path for open reached destinations, avoiding normalize_overworld_state_for_runtime, _resolve_post_move_interaction, _finalize_action_result, _attach_post_action_recap, and _post_action_tile_context on that hot path.

Descriptor destination interactions use destination_interaction_fast_path and dispatch resource/artifact/encounter/town by descriptor kind/id/tile instead of broad active-tile discovery; unsafe descriptors report fallback reasons and use full_revalidation fallback.
Route decision construction now records interception_ms and avoids runtime normalization for selected-destination-only interception checks without enabling the global fallback scan.
F3/profile JSONL movement_rules details now expose cached_execution_mode, post_action_recap_skipped, scenario_eval_skipped, interaction_dispatch_mode, and fallback_reason.

Post-move current-tile selected-route refresh uses the same minimal current action surface instead of rebuilding full rich route destination UI just to show hold/current.
Adjacent open-tile direct clicks now use cached selected-route execution/open_fast_path while non-open adjacent destinations keep the existing direct move/interaction fallback.

nonGoals: Do not change map generation, templates, player counts, object density/counts, object placement, objectives, save schema, renderer art semantics, or pathing/body/visit contracts.

Do not implement separate stale enemy-sighting, AI memory, or grey-current-visibility gameplay layers in this slice.
completionEvidence: OverworldRules now normalizes visible_tiles as a duplicate compatibility/cache of explored_tiles, merges legacy visible true cells into explored during normalization, and makes is_tile_visible read permanent explored coverage.

Routine try_move reveals current player hero/site scout sources before post-move interaction and finalizes with bounded incremental fog expansion instead of the previous transient full-map current-visibility rebuild path.
OverworldMapView validation terrain state now reports explored_visible for explored tiles, while unexplored tiles remain hidden.

plannedSlices.title: Overworld Full-Route Movement Model
completionCriteria: Path preview precomputes cost for the full selected route and partitions reachable versus out-of-movement tiles.
Mouse/primary route confirmation executes movement along the selected route to the farthest reachable tile in the current movement budget.
Routes do not pass through blockers or intermediate interactions; interaction resolution only occurs when the interaction destination is actually reached.
Fog remains HoMM-style permanent explored visibility and expands along traversed route steps.
F3/debug command buckets identify full-route execution and retain movement timing evidence.
nonGoals: Do not change map generation, templates, player counts, object density/counts, object placement, objectives, save schema, renderer art semantics, or object body/visit contracts.
Do not add primitive/debug rendering, hidden downscale, content cuts, fake animation, or broad save/render/content migrations.
Do not weaken blocker, body tile, visit tile, or interaction destination contracts.
completionEvidence: OverworldRules exposes route_movement_preview and try_move_along_route. Preview normalizes a full route, totals cost, partitions reachable and out-of-movement tiles, and reports the farthest reachable final tile.

try_move_along_route validates that a route starts at the active hero, is contiguous, stays in bounds, avoids blockers, and does not pass through intermediate interactions; it consumes one movement point per traversed tile, updates the active hero final position, reveals fog along route steps, and only resolves the destination interaction when that destination is reached.

OverworldShell primary route confirmation now executes the selected route to the farthest reachable tile instead of advancing only one adjacent step, while single-step try_move remains available for keyboard/direct movement callers.

OverworldShell and OverworldMapView route selection/path building no longer reject cached-but-undiscovered passable destinations, but still reject blocked destinations and paths through intermediate interactions.

OverworldMapView draws the reachable route segment in the normal route style and continues the planned out-of-movement segment in the blocked/red style; validation_view_metrics exposes the route preview partition.
F3 command timing now records full_route_execute with route_execution_lookup and movement_rules buckets for primary route confirmation.

Focused route regression evidence: full-route preview total_steps=9, reachable_steps=4, unreachable_steps=5; confirmation executed_steps=4 to final tile 4,1, consumed movement to 0, preserved the unreachable destination, and reported full_route_execute total_command_ms=2121.124, route_execution_lookup_ms=0.004, movement_rules_ms=48.202.
plannedSlices.notes: Animation remains prepared through route_steps/route_execution payloads only; no fake tile-by-tile animation was added in this slice.

completionCriteria: Routine generated-map movement/selection avoids semantically redundant full generated-map/session normalization and broad action rebuild work.

nonGoals: Do not change map size, template behavior, player-count behavior, density, object counts, generation semantics, pathing semantics, renderer art semantics, saves, objectives, or balance.
Do not add debug/primitive render shortcuts, hidden downscale, content cuts, or fake speedups.
Do not weaken body-tile blocking, visit-tile passability/reachability, or body-click-to-visit-tile resolution contracts.
completionEvidence: Runtime generated-map normalization is now signature-gated and marked fresh after full normalization, movement, and action finalization, so routine movement and refresh read scopes do not redo full generated-map/session normalization when the normalized state is already current.
Movement, resource collection, artifact collection, and town capture use the runtime-normalized path while preserving final fog/scenario refresh and save schema.

plannedSlices.title: Generated Small Object Density Corrective
completionEvidence: Focused Small distribution report profiles live interactables by kind/source, start-distance rings, nearest POIs, visible/explored density, road proximity, reachable counts, runtime reward-reference materialization, and largest empty reachable 8x8 windows.

Baseline diagnosis showed 34 live reachable interactables, only 3 mid-ring interactables, a 64-cell empty reachable 8x8 window, 8 staged reward references without live resource nodes, and no unreachable live interactables; the issue was under-materialized route/frontier rewards plus clustering, not true map size, template, player count, fog, or blocked tiles.

Generator now materializes supported existing route reward-band resource sites into normal live resource nodes and adds bounded Small compact density support only for large reachable empty windows using existing low-tier reward cache content.

Final focused metrics: 49 live reachable interactables, 7 early-ring, 10 mid-ring, 14 frontier-ring, 38 near-road interactables, 0 unreachable interactables, 15 live materialized reward resource nodes, and largest empty reachable 8x8 window reduced to 43 cells while preserving 36x36 Small size, compact template/profile, three players, objectives, footprint interactions, and normal art path.
plannedSlices.notes: Completed after AcOrP Small-map playtest found sparse meaningful points of interest in revealed/nearby playable space. Scope stayed limited to distribution and materialized reachable POIs; no true-size/template/player-count/object-semantic changes or global count inflation.

entries.title: Raise native generated decoration/blocker footprint coverage and add a fill gate
entries.evidence: Native C++ generated packages now use terrain-biased large decoration/blocker bodies from authored content proportions, reserve full body occupancy for later town/guard placement, and expose fill coverage metrics. The focused fill report compares HoMM3-re rand_trn scale, authored blocker scale, attached pre-fix package metrics, and sampled native output. The attached 72x72 package fails the new medium floor at 2.78% deco/blocker coverage; the same config regenerated through native generation reaches 24.96%. Sampled small/medium/large/XL coverage reached 24.77%, 33.35%, 26.11%, and 21.55% deco/blocker coverage, with road/package surfaces intact.

entries.summary: Added an empirical spatial placement comparison report that parses the owner-attached HoMM3 H3M tile/object tables and compares spatial density, nearest-neighbor, road-adjacency, and low-content-region metrics against native owner-like generation.
entries.evidence: Owner H3M parsed as version 28, 72x72, 297 object definitions, 496 placed objects, 184 road tiles. Native owner-like output passes the new spatial gate with 344 objects, 240 road tiles, reward nonempty 6x6 cells 26, reward average road distance 5.287, reward within-4-road ratio 0.5662, and largest low-content region 3.
entries.remainingGap: Does not claim full HoMM3-re parity; exact object/art/template/byte placement and owner water/land shape parity remain open.

entries.summary: Reduced a clear native spatial skew by changing non-town zone object placement from anchor-ring clustering to deterministic coarse-grid scatter within each owning zone.
entries.evidence: Owner-like all-content quadrant CV improved from 0.5123 before the change to 0.3560 after; reward quadrant CV from 0.5849 to 0.3499; guard quadrant CV from 0.8008 to 0.5968; largest low-content 6x6 region from 4 cells to 3 cells.
entries.remainingGap: The native map still differs from the owner H3M in land/water distribution, object count, exact decoration clumping, and exact road/object placement semantics.

entries.summary: Added a land-normalized object/category density report that parses the owner-attached HoMM3 H3M and compares owner metrics against native owner-like C++ output after the land/water fix.
entries.evidence: Owner parsed at 1,948 land, 3,236 water, 496 objects, 184 roads. Native final report passes at 2,316 land, 2,868 water, 488 objects, 240 roads, total object density 0.828x owner, decoration/impassable density 0.804x owner, reward/resource 1.040x owner, guard 1.434x owner, package object count 488.
entries.remainingGap: No full HoMM3-re parity claim; native still lacks owner other-object category equivalents and does not match exact object table, placement, art, or H3M bytes.

entries.summary: Implemented one bounded native C++ owner-like islands density improvement by adding compact decorative/impassable instance scatter for the 72x72 translated Small Ring islands profile.
entries.evidence: Native owner-like baseline moved from 344 objects and 0.330x decoration/impassable density to 488 objects and 0.804x decoration/impassable density while land/water shape and spatial placement gates pass.
entries.remainingGap: The supplement is bounded to the owner-like profile and is not global random inflation or exact HoMM3 object-table parity.

completedImplementationEvidence.title: Native RMG Owner Medium Islands Runtime Support
evidence: Medium islands spatial comparison passed for profile 001 with 8 towns, 4 zones, 496 objects, 61 guards, owner-compared support enabled, and full parity still false.
Package object-only breadth report passed the new owner_medium_islands_001 case with 8 towns, 61 guards, 496 objects, 206 loaded road tiles, and zero object-only all-town or cross-zone town routes.
Full parity gate passed with the new Medium islands case runtime-adopted only as owner-compared not-full-parity output.
Package adoption and authoritative replay reports passed after excluding diagnostic runtime phase timing from replay identity signatures.
Random-map menu wiring and skirmish UI save/replay reports passed, preserving the four-template player-facing surface and not exposing islands globally.

completedImplementationEvidence.title: Native RMG Runtime Authority Parity Gate Repair
evidence: Full parity gate report passed with legacy compact non-authoritative and owner-compared translated defaults runtime-authoritative without full parity.
Owner-compared package adoption now requires remaining parity slices for full parity, islands support, and broad owner comparison.

completedImplementationEvidence.title: Native RMG Legacy Compact Launch Block
evidence: Player setup retry UX report passed after asserting explicit compact launch is blocked with native_rmg_legacy_compact_launch_blocked.
Maps-folder package browser integration report passed with compact fixture rejected and translated package accepted.
Random-map skirmish UI save/replay report passed on native package provenance and package-backed restore.
Final writeout export/save schema report passed while keeping compact generation as a legacy export fixture outside production launch.
Random-map scenario load smoke passed after treating maps-folder package entries separately from archived authored scenarios.

native-rmg-generalized-policy-regate-101: Medium two-level islands catalog-auto profile cleanup: focused m_4players_2levels_islands export wrote 1/1 package with native validation pass; fast audit improved production-gap severity from 2175 to 803, total object delta from +476 to +185, road delta from +54 to -5, terrain-blocked delta from -724 to +178, and kept guarded-route delta at 0 while reducing object-only reachable town pairs from 15 to 10. Merged 18-case evidence at .artifacts/rmg_native_batch_export_medium_islands_2level_profile_merged passed rmg_quick_validation and rmg_python_validation_gate with 18/18 matches and 0 parse/native/density/policy/topology/coverage/closure-shape gaps; production_ready remains false because exact road/terrain/category/route shape gaps remain.

Medium two-level normal-water catalog-auto profile cleanup: focused m_normalw_4players_2levels export wrote 1/1 package with native validation pass; fast audit improved production-gap severity from 2027 to 429, total object delta from -663 to -4, road delta from -35 to 0, terrain-blocked delta from -141 to -40, category absolute delta from 663 to 10, reward count from 178 to 414 versus owner 416, and guard count from 104 to 191 versus owner 194. Merged 18-case evidence at .artifacts/rmg_native_batch_export_medium_normalwater_2level_profile_merged passed rmg_quick_validation and rmg_python_validation_gate with 18/18 matches and 0 parse/native/density/policy/topology/coverage/closure-shape gaps; production_ready remains false because exact road/terrain/category/route shape gaps remain, with xl_islands now the top blocker.

XL one-level islands catalog-auto profile cleanup: focused xl_islands export wrote 1/1 package with native validation pass; fast audit improved production-gap severity from 1868 to 172, total object delta from +93 to +2, road delta from -52 to 0, terrain-blocked delta from +1505 to +139, category absolute delta from 193 to 6, guard delta from +139 to -1, and reward delta from -49 to 0. Merged 18-case evidence at .artifacts/rmg_native_batch_export_xl_islands_profile_merged passed rmg_quick_validation and rmg_python_validation_gate with 18/18 matches and 0 parse/native/density/policy/topology/coverage/closure-shape gaps; production_ready remains false because exact town/route/road-component shape gaps remain, with l_normalwater_randomplayers now the top blocker.
